ective, spiri
tual. One is occupied with invisible affairs. Superficial reli
gion, outward pomp and show, meaningless extravagance and
vanity all these are vehemently opposed. Such is the life of
Mrs. Annie Besant too.
But such clear-cut cases are rare and we frequently find
planets inclining this way and that at the same time. Yet the
same features will hold though to a lesser extent. But in these
cases nealry half the houses are falling vacant. And a vacant
house is usually slurred over by our professionals. But we
have to note that the presence of a planet in a sign only means
that the affairs denoted by that sign are particularly empha
sised. As such the vacant signs are as much significant as the
filled ones.
The central house at the eastern part of the horoscope
reveals the personality of the individual. This is the Ascen
dant. But this is always related to the two adjoining houses,
the twelfth and the second. The twelfth house governs the
